text:
Once upon a sunny Tuesday morning, in a quaint little town known for its annual flower parade, three friends decided to celebrate the festivities with a simple picnic by the riverside. Mia, the planner; Lucas, the jester; and Ava, the artist.

Lucas bounded out of bed first, ready for something wild. “Let's make this the strangest picnic ever!” he declared, donning a rubber chicken costume for good measure, attracting giggles from the sleeping companions.

Ava, wrapping art supplies in recycled paper to decorate their spot, looked charmingly disheveled in her overalls and floral apron. Mia, always the organiser, tied colorful ribbons around their makeshift tent, ensuring the picnic theme was as whimsical as their spirits.

As they ventured to the riverside, Lucas’s antics caught the attention of a flock of local birds who seemed fascinated by the rubber chicken. Unperturbed, Lucas twirled, his costume shining in the sunlight, and the
